title: Preserve Every Customer
subtitle: Use LEFT JOIN and interpret unmatched columns as NULL
learning_objective: Return every customer even when no order exists.
why: Customer lists often need to show inactivity rather than silently dropping customers with no transactions.
prompt: Return `customer_id`, `customer_name`, `order_id`, and `status` for every customer and any matching orders. Order by `customer_id`, then `order_id`.
expected_result: 8 rows. Evergreen Health and Frostline Labs each appear once with `NULL` order values.
build_steps: Place `customers` on the left because every customer must remain.
Order by customer and order IDs.
explanation: A left join preserves each customer. Customers with multiple orders create multiple rows; customers with no orders create one row with NULL order columns.
common_mistakes: Starting from `orders`, which cannot preserve customers with no orders.
Changing the join to INNER JOIN.
Filtering `o.status` in WHERE and accidentally removing NULL rows.
hints: The preserved table must appear before `LEFT JOIN`.
Do not add a WHERE clause.
takeaways: LEFT JOIN preserves the left table.
One-to-many matches can create more than one row per preserved record.
reflection_questions: Why do Apex Studios and Cascade Media appear twice?
Where did the NULL values come from?
starter_sql:
-- Write your answer below.
-- Use the task, expected output, and dataset details above.

SELECT
    -- requested columns
FROM customers;
solution_explanation: The customer table is intentionally preserved, so unmatched customers remain with NULL order fields.
solution_walkthrough: The output starts with all six customers.
Matching orders expand customers 1 and 3 into two rows each.
Their right-side order columns are filled with NULL.
